normalized_title: Development Control Policy 3.4 Subdivision of Rural Land
scope_summary: Sets out the principles the WAPC applies when determining applications for the subdivision (including amalgamation and survey-strata) of rural-zoned land throughout Western Australia, consistent with SPP 2.5 Rural Planning. Creation of new or smaller rural lots is by exception, with subdivision considered only in defined circumstances: significant physical divisions, subdivision for other (non-rural-living) purposes, property rationalisation, conservation of heritage or biodiversity, and homestead lots within the mapped Homestead Lot Policy Area. Rural living subdivision is expressly excluded and dealt with under SPP 2.5 cl 5.3. Supersedes the March 2012 version of DC 3.4.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: homestead lots excised from farm holdings in the Homestead Lot Policy Area

key_numeric_standards.topic: rural living lot size range (lots created via property rationalisation)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: rural living sized lots resulting from boundary rationalisation

key_numeric_standards.topic: conservation lot size threshold triggering adequacy assessment
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: conservation lots (agency advice required on adequacy if smaller)

key_numeric_standards.topic: maximum homestead lots per rural lot (one-off; more where multiple approved dwellings exist)
